title: In regards to all the hate and issues with 30 here
display_title: In regards to all the hate and issues with 30 here
wikitext:
[]: In regards to all the hate and issues with 3.0 here...             Quote Post by Th3Marauder » Tue Jul 23, 2013 3:08 am Imagine it's 2002, you're sitting at home, doing whatever people did in 2002, and the door bursts open and your best friends storms in. "Whoa!" you exclaim, "Whats wrong?" "I'm just pissed off man!" he answers "What? Why?" you ask "I just got back from the cinemas, I just watched The Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers!" he explains "..." you just sit for a moment, "Why are you pissed about that?" The friend sits down and starts ranting "Ok so after Fellowship everything was cool, but now there are so many unanswered questions and stuff that just doesn't make sense! Like how did Smeagol end up as Gollum? What's going to happen with the Ring? What's going to happen with Saruman? Will Frodo and Sam make it to Mordor? I mean they were right there, and now they're off walking through the woods! I mean what the hell! Way to gives us questions with no answers Jackson!" You sit dumbstruck for a second. "You..." you stammer, "You know that Return of the King, the final film in the trilogy, is coming out next year, right?" "Yeah but that's not the point! I want answers now!" he shouts "Literally everything you just asked about will be answered, you really can't just wait 12 months!" you ask "No goddammit!" the friend jumps up and storms out of the room. What I'm trying to get at here is maybe we should wait for FINAL before complaining about unanswered questions.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Jul 23, 2013 3:39 am I think you're missing the point of what made 3.0 so polarizing. To retake your example : let's imagine that at the end of The Two Towers , you have Saruman's army defeated at Helm's Deep, Merry and Pippin are kicking Saruman's butt with the Ents and Frodon, Sam and Gollum leaving Osgiliath to go to the Mordor, the last step of their journey... and then comes The Return of the King , Frodon is captured by the Gondor that threats him like a criminal, then you learn that 14 years have passed since The Two Towers , Sauron got back the Ring and ravaged the world with his powers back and the remaining humans, elves, dwarfs and hobbits are desperately fighting for their life, and Frodon is considered as the responsible for Sauron retrieving the Ring... but no one explains exactly how Frodo is supposed to have given back the Ring to Sauron. Of course there is the possibility that everything will be explained later in a fourth movie, or maybe not since the plot must go forward, but in any case it's still frustrating that 14 years passed under our noses like that, and that everything the good guys fought for, every companions Frodo made during his journey was for naught since he lost everything : he failed his mission, his native Shire was burned to the ground, all of his friends are dead and the few remaining treats him like a war criminal. That's what made 3.0 so polarizing. Now objectively, you have to give credit to Anno for this incredibly ballsy move that will ensure that the Rebuild project will be remembered as far more than "NGE with shiny 3D CGI", especially since I got the feeling that it will be hard to sell as much goodies based on Q than with NGE and the previous films. It's like in those post-apocalyptic movies or video games where you get a glimpse at the beginning of the world before everything goes to hell, it gives you a point of reference of how it was "before", but you don't have time to get attached to the "before", but here in Rebuild the "glimpse" lasted for half of the quadrilogy, you didn't had any idea that a post-apocalyptic setting was on its way and that the third movie will be a prologue for said setting. Personally, I'm waiting for Final to give my definitive judgment about the Rebuild, since Q clearly serves as a prologue for the final installment.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Tue Jul 23, 2013 3:48 am @Mariachi THANK YOU. People honestly need to stop treating those who had problems with Q's narrative as though they are behaving like little children. There are legitimate points to be made on both sides of the argument.

[]:             Quote Post by Th3Marauder » Tue Jul 23, 2013 4:15 am I admit the metaphor isn't perfect, but the specifics aren't the point. I understand that people have issues with 3.0, what I'm saying is simply that we are complaining and moaning about something before we've been given the full story.

[]:             Quote Post by Yaywalter » Tue Jul 23, 2013 5:28 am Guy Nacks wrote: @Mariachi THANK YOU. People honestly need to stop treating those who had problems with Q's narrative as though they are behaving like little children. There are legitimate points to be made on both sides of the argument.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Jul 23, 2013 6:35 am In regards to all the hate and issues with 3.0 here... this is nothing to what 2.0 caused. Seriously. We haven't even had to ban people from the Rebuild subforum this time around.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Jul 23, 2013 6:48 am Well, only for spoilers! (temp, of course)

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Jul 23, 2013 6:55 am Mr. Tines wrote: In regards to all the hate and issues with 3.0 here... this is nothing to what 2.0 caused. Seriously. We haven't even had to ban people from the Rebuild subforum this time around.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Jul 23, 2013 8:07 am Mr. Tines wrote: In regards to all the hate and issues with 3.0 here... this is nothing to what 2.0 caused. Seriously. We haven't even had to ban people from the Rebuild subforum this time around.

[]:             Quote Post by aboose » Tue Jul 23, 2013 8:13 am This may be too meta, but the polarizing aspects of 2.0 and 3.0 (you either hate 2.0 and love 3.0, vice versa, or hate both) are what make rebuild feel so much like the original series to me. People getting mad and not understanding. Sounds like NGE to me.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Tue Jul 23, 2013 8:21 am OP's gonna have to give me a better example cause I don't like nor have I seen Lord of The Rings. It's one thing to leave things unanswered so they're going to be explained in FINAL. It's another thing entirely to leave things unanswered that doesn't need to be answered in FINAL and could be answered in 3.0. What happened to the 11th Angel? What happened to Unit 07, 9, 10, and 11? What went down in Dogma? Where is Neo-NERV and WILLE getting the resources for all this shit they have? You can answer these questions in 3.0, but they either decided to save it for FINAL (for God knows why reasons), or they're not going to answer them at all which only a few questions I can see being justified in not answering them. But questions like "Where did they get the money for this shit?" being unanswered is just a way to dodge the point so you can continue to watch new and improved Eva's beat the shit out of each other with cool weapons and other fancy crap.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jul 23, 2013 11:32 am ElMariachi wrote: It was that bad when 2.0 was released?!

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Tue Jul 23, 2013 11:44 am I don't like the time skip because if you do that, at least bring something interesting in terms of story and character development. Story? The movie ends in a status quo, nothing really happened. Character development? Barely any. The interactions between Kaworu and Shinji aren't more elaborated than what happened in the original episode 24, and it lasted only 20 minutes in the original series. ALL the other characters are neglected and we don't know anything about them. If you want to surprise people with a time skip or something like that 3 years after the previous movie, back it up and propose something strong and interesting. I don't think it's the case with that movie. It doesn't move the story forward, what does is the time skip, and we don't know what happened. So yeah, I'm not super happy about that whole stuff, even though I like the movie for what it is.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Tue Jul 23, 2013 11:46 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: These are just 2 examples, but Yep. It was.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Tue Jul 23, 2013 12:13 pm I love the timeskip & everything it brought. So I don't see a problem.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Jul 23, 2013 12:15 pm TheFriskyIan wrote: I never saw the point in the first thread.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Jul 23, 2013 12:35 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: These are just 2 examples, but Yep. It was.

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Tue Jul 23, 2013 12:37 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: These are just 2 examples, but Yep. It was.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Tue Jul 23, 2013 12:46 pm Yaywalter wrote: I've yet to see a legitimate argument about why Q's time skip was actually a bad thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Jul 23, 2013 2:36 pm aboose wrote: (you either hate 2.0 and love 3.0, vice versa, or hate both)
